ddls:
CREATE TABLE "beacons" (
  "beaconid" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each beacon */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name or description of the location where the beacon is installed */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the beacon was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the beacon (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "beacon_model" TEXT /* Model of the beacon */,
  "battery_level" REAL /* Battery level of the beacon */,
  "last_battery_check" TEXT /* Last time the battery was checked */,
  "maintenance_due_date" TEXT /* Date when maintenance is due */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("beaconid")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., facility manager, technician, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "beaconid" INTEGER /* Reference to the beacon related to the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_beaconid FOREIGN KEY ("beaconid") REFERENCES beacons ("beaconid")
)

CREATE TABLE "environmental_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum acceptable value for the metric */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum acceptable value for the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting (e.g., max_temperature) */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the environmental metric associated with the setting */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., upper, lower) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_settings_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES environmental_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who generated the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., usage, environmental) */,
  "report_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the report */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path of the generated report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "action_datetime" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action was performed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the access */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the action (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
